zh.lifeSummary: 當Abigail Ford於1619年10月8日之前出生在英國英格蘭多瑟特布利波特時，她的父親Thomas Ford29歲，母親Elizabeth Charde28歲。她於1635年與Elder John Strong在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼辛加姆結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她去世於1688年7月6日在美國麻薩諸塞州哈普爾諾薩普頓，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州哈普爾諾薩普頓布里奇 史崔特 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Abigail Ford于1619年10月8日之前出生在英国英格兰多瑟特布利波特时，她的父亲Thomas Ford29岁，母亲Elizabeth Charde28岁。她于1635年与Elder John Strong在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼辛加姆结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和8个女儿的父母。她去世于1688年7月6日在美国马萨诸塞州哈普爾諾薩普頓，埋葬在美国马萨诸塞州哈普爾諾薩普頓布里奇 史崔特 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Ford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived near a ford (Middle English, Old English ford), or a habitational name from one of the many places called with this word, such as Ford (Durham, Herefordshire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Sussex), Ford in Sefton (Lancashire), Ford in Crediton and Ford in Holcombe Rogus (both Devon), Ford in Litton and Ford in Wiveliscombe (both Somerset).

Irish: Anglicized form (quasi-translation) of various Gaelic names, for example MacGiolla na Naomh ‘son of Gilla na Naomh’ (a personal name meaning ‘servant of the saints’), Mac Conshámha ‘son of Conshnámha’ (a personal name composed of the elements con ‘dog’ + snámh ‘to swim’), in all of which the final syllable was wrongly thought to be áth ‘ford’, and Ó Fuar(th)áin (see Foran ).

variants.name: Forth
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived by a ford, Middle High German vort, vurt, Middle Low German vorde ‘ford, entrance way’, or a habitational name from a place in Franconia or in former Pomerania (now Poland) named Forth.

variants.name: Forde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Declan, Dermot, Aine, Fionnuala, Liam, Nuala.
English and Irish: variant of   Ford   1 and 2. This is a very common spelling in Ireland.
Norwegian (Førde): habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads named Førde (there are eleven on the west coast), from Old Norse fyrthi, dative of fjǫrthr ‘fjord’.

variants.name: Foran
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fuartháin ‘descendant of Fuarthán’, a personal name derived from fuar ‘cold’. The word fuarán also means ‘spring’ or ‘pool’. The form Fuarthán was understood as fuarathán ‘cold little ford’, and the name has thus sometimes been translated into English as Ford .

nameMeanings.name: Abigail
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘father of exaltation’ in Hebrew, borne by one of King David's wives, who had earlier been married to Nabal (1 Samuel 25:3), and by the mother of Absalom's captain Amasa (2 Samuel 1:25). The name first came into general use in Britain in the 16th century, under Puritan influence. It was a common name in literature for a lady's maid, for example in  Beaumont  and  Fletcher's  play The Scornful Lady (  1616 ). The biblical Abigail refers to herself as ‘thy servant’ in addressing King David. In Ireland this name has traditionally been used as an Anglicized form of Gobnat , although the reasons for this are not clear. It was popular in the 17th century, especially among Puritans and Nonconformists, and has again enjoyed considerable favour since the 1990s.

historicEvents.description: Oldest grave seen in the memorial list

Bridge Street Cemetery is located at 156 Bridge Street (Route 9) in Northampton next Lampron Park and the Bridge Street Elementary School.  Established in 1661, it is the oldest cemetery in Northampton.  The City of Northampton oversees the cemetery.

en.lifeSummary: When Abigail Ford was born before 8 October 1619, in Bridport, Dorset, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Ford, was 29 and her mother, Elizabeth Charde, was 28. She married Elder John Strong in 1635, in Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 8 daughters. She died on 6 July 1688, in Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts, United States, and was buried in Bridge Street Cemetery, Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts, United States.

ko.lifeSummary: Abigail Ford(이)가 1619년 10월 8일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 브리드포트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Ford(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Charde(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1635년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 잉함에서 Elder John Strong(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1688년 7월 6일 미국 매사추세츠 주 함프실르 노르섬프턴에서 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 함프실르 노르섬프턴 브리지 스트릿 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Abigail Ford nasceu antes 8 de outubro de 1619, em Bridport, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas Ford, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Charde, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Elder John Strong em 1635, em Hingham, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 8 filhas. Ela faleceu em 6 de julho de 1688, em Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América, e foi sepultada em Bridge Street Cemetery, Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Abigail Ford родилась перед 8 октябрь 1619 г. в местности бридпорт, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Ford было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Charde, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Elder John Strong в 1635 г. в местности хингхам, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 8 дочерей. Она умерла 6 июля 1688 г. в местности нортамптон, хампшир, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты и была похоронена в местности бридж стрит кеметири, нортамптон, хампшир, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.
